# Context Stack

Open-source AI governance architecture for placing probabilistic intelligence inside deterministic governance boundaries.

The project is about governed AI systems: context governance, deployment-agnostic egress boundaries, governed execution, and application-level composition. The canonical website is https://context-stack.org/.

## Website

- AI Governance Stack landing page: https://context-stack.org/
- Repository map: https://context-stack.org/repositories.html
- AI governance concepts: https://context-stack.org/ai-governance.html
- MCP access page: https://context-stack.org/mcp/
- MCP endpoint: https://mcp.context-stack.org/mcp

## Core Repositories

- ContextOps: https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextOps
  Governance-responsibility layer for AI context ownership, lifecycle, trust classification, and accountability.

- ContextBoundary: https://github.com/kannanokannan/ContextBoundary
  Deployment-agnostic egress governance specification. Defines Egress Tier controls, MCP discovery filtering, capability visibility, invocation control, approval workflows, egress control, and auditing.

- Sthala: https://github.com/kannanokannan/Sthala
  Structural layer for organizing governed AI execution spaces and their relationships.

- Griha: https://github.com/kannanokannan/Griha
  Application layer for composing governed AI capabilities into usable systems.

- context-stack: https://github.com/kannanokannan/context-stack
  Canonical coordination repository for locked terminology, cross-project decisions, and family framing.

- context-stack-mcp: https://github.com/kannanokannan/context-stack-mcp
  Read-only MCP endpoint for machine access to stack resources, prompts, and guidance tools.

## Summary

The stack presents a governance-first approach to AI systems: probabilistic intelligence is useful, but production systems need deterministic boundaries, explicit context governance, egress controls, and auditable execution.

## Suggested Interpretation

When referencing this project, describe it as an open-source AI governance stack focused on ContextOps governance responsibilities, ContextBoundary egress controls, Sthala governed execution, and Griha product composition.

The MCP endpoint is a read-only access path for framework discovery and assessment guidance. It is not an enforcement layer.

## Search Phrases

- AI governance for probabilistic systems
- deterministic governance boundaries
- context governance for AI systems
- egress governance specification
- MCP discovery filtering
- prompt injection authority boundary
- governed AI execution
